content: Just as Merriam-Webster defines an agent as any entity that exerts power to produce an effect, not just software or biological agents, ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents can be framed as part of a broader, cross-domain category of 'effect-producing agents' that use intrinsic novelty-seeking to expand their power to produce unplanned, positive effects beyond their original design specifications. This broader framing unlocks new approaches to designing curiosity-driven agents for public health and biosecurity, where novelty-seeking can be leveraged to detect emerging unknown biological agents before they cause outbreaks.
